payload.text: I still see Wylie band Command. Get rid of that and operational directivy

**Locating Items for Removal** I've identified "Wylie Band Command" as an entry within `constants.ts`. My next step is to examine that file and proceed with its removal. This should be a straightforward task, but I'll need to confirm any dependencies before proceeding. I'll make sure to verify all references before the change. **Pinpointing Elements for Removal** I've located "Operational Directive 001" within `JOURNAL_ARTICLES` in `constants.ts`, specifically as a `p` element. I'm leaning toward removing the entire `p` element or simply deleting the text "Operational Directive 001" based on the prompt's wording to "get rid of that and operational directivy". I'm also confirming the `TOOLS` array within the same file, to review and remove the identified "Wylie Band Command". **Analyzing Text for Removal** I'm now focusing on removing the specified text. The prompt mentions "operational directivy," which I believe is a typo for "directive." I've identified the "Operational Directive: Lead with Vision" text in `components/Hero.tsx` and have to remove it. I've also checked `JOURNAL_ARTICLES` in `constants.ts` and will remove the content starting with `

Operational Directive 001

`. **Confirming Elements for Removal** I'm now certain about removing "Operational Directive 001" from `constants.ts` and "Operational Directive: Lead with Vision" from `components/Hero.tsx` as both are targets, and "Wylie Band Command" from `TOOLS` array. I will proceed with removal of all the specified elements to ensure the user's intent is fully satisfied. I'm focusing on the implementation details now. **Implementing Removal Tasks** I'm now going to focus on removing `Wylie Band Command` from `TOOLS` within `constants.ts`. I'll also eliminate the paragraph for `Operational Directive 001` from the `JOURNAL_ARTICLES` content, paying close attention to its structure, as it's built with `React.createElement`. Lastly, I will revise `components/Hero.tsx` to remove the line concerning the operational directive, specifically ensuring proper handling of the line break. **Removing Unnecessary Content** I'm now zeroing in on removing the specified text and elements. I'll get rid of the first paragraph containing "Operational Directive 001" in `constants.ts`. I also verified that the goal of the prompt is a removal of the paragraph element, and not all occurrences of "operational directives." I will also remove `p3` from the `TOOLS` array in that same file. Finally, I will address that line in `components/Hero.tsx`. **Finalizing Content Removal** I've determined that since "Wylie Band Command" corresponds to the "Community" category, removing the tool necessitates removing "Community" from the `categories` array in `components/ProductGrid.tsx`. I've confirmed these changes require modifications to `constants.ts` (for the tool and journal text) and `components/Hero.tsx` (for the hero text), along with the addition of a change to `components/ProductGrid.tsx` for removing the now-unused category. I'm preparing to generate the final XML changes. **Refining Implementation Steps** I'm now extending the scope to encompass cleanup after removing "Wylie Band Command". Since "Wylie Band Command" mapped to the "Community" category, removing that tool leaves the category empty, therefore I should remove the "Community" entry. This impacts `components/ProductGrid.tsx` and the `category` union type in `types.ts`. I will update both to avoid an unused category. I will also remove the `React.createElement` line for operational directive. **Executing Removal Procedures** I'm now going to remove the identified content. I'll eliminate "Operational Directive 001" from the journal, the associated line in Hero.tsx, and the 'Community' category and its associated type definition in ProductGrid.tsx and types.ts. I am verifying those changes now, and preparing to finalize the commit.
The following changes remove the "Wylie Band Command" tool, the "Community" category associated with it, and the references to "Operational Directive" in both the Hero section and Journal entries.

entries.description: Removed Wylie Band Command from TOOLS and Operational Directive 001 text from Journal.

diffs.replacement:
/**
 * @license
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
*/

import React from 'react';
import { Tool, JournalArticle } from './types';

export const TOOLS: Tool[] = [
  {
    id: 'p1',
    name: 'Northbrand Aperture',
    tagline: 'Precision Optics & Visual Memory.',
    description: 'Leica-centric photography focusing on high-contrast, shallow depth of field, and monochromatic West Texas landscapes.',
    longDescription: 'Photography is an instrument of vision. The Aperture project explores the desolation of the Texas plains through a technical lens, prioritizing composition rigor and the "Leica Look"—sharp subject isolation with creamy, atmospheric bokeh.',
    category: 'Photography',
    imageUrl: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1510127034890-ba27508e9f1c?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=1000',
    link: 'https://www.instagram.com/brondolfr.lo',
    features: ['Subject Isolation', 'High Dynamic Range', 'Monochrome Focus']
  },
  {
    id: 'p2',
    name: 'Control Tempo: VA Navigator',
    tagline: 'Aviation-Grade Logistics for Veterans.',
    description: 'A dedicated command center for navigating VA benefits, healthcare, and transition logistics with military precision.',
    longDescription: 'Applying the "Prevent Error" principle to the often-chaotic VA system. This navigator uses standardized checklists and failure-mode analysis to help veterans track claims and healthcare outcomes with decimal-point accuracy.',
    category: 'Military/VA',
    imageUrl: 'https://va-healthcare.vercel.app/og-image.png', // Fallback or site-specific
    link: 'https://va-healthcare.vercel.app/',
    features: ['FMEA Claim Logic', 'Standardized Checklists', 'Secure Tracking']
  },
  {
    id: 'p4',
    name: 'Retirement Scenario Inverter',
    tagline: 'Stress-Testing the Future.',
    description: 'Strategic dashboard designed to identify and neutralize failure points in long-term financial planning.',
    longDescription: 'Most tools show growth; we show risk. Using the "Invert Problems" mental model, this curator allows users to stress-test their retirement against inflation spikes, market crashes, and healthcare variables to ensure total system integrity.',
    category: 'Retirement',
    imageUrl: 'https://retirement-curator.vercel.app/preview.png',
    link: 'https://retirement-curator.vercel.app/',
    features: ['Inflation Stress-Testing', 'Failure-Point Scenarios', 'Precision Projection']
  },
  {
    id: 'p5',
    name: 'CRM J-MATS',
    tagline: 'Logistical Force Multiplier.',
    description: 'Operational tracking system for military skill sets and transition logistics.',
    longDescription: 'J-MATS acts as a technical force multiplier. It translates military service records into civilian career technical specs, externalizing data to a central source of truth (Supabase) to prevent skill-set loss during transition.',
    category: 'Military/VA',
    imageUrl: 'https://crm-jmats.vercel.app/dashboard.png',
    link: 'https://crm-jmats.vercel.app/',
    features: ['Skill Translation Logic', 'Secure Data Vault', 'Logistics Dashboard']
  },
  {
    id: 'p6',
    name: 'Northbrand Dev Lab',
    tagline: 'Operational Efficiency in Code.',
    description: 'Frontend engineering research focused on Vercel-deployed React and TypeScript experiments.',
    longDescription: 'The laboratory for Northbrand Collective’s code DNA. Every deployment follows a "No-Waste" engineering approach, focusing on atomic components, accessible UI, and sub-second render times.',
    category: 'Development',
    imageUrl: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1498050108023-c5249f4df085?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=1000',
    link: 'https://github.com/brondolfr',
    features: ['Vercel High-Availability', 'Atomic Design', 'Rigorous Testing']
  }
];

export const JOURNAL_ARTICLES: JournalArticle[] = [
    {
        id: 1,
        title: "The 9 Principles of Northbrand",
        date: "May 10, 2025",
        excerpt: "Operational logic for a visual and technical life.",
        image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1542038784456-1ea8e935640e?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=1000",
        content: React.createElement(React.Fragment, null,
            React.createElement("p", { className: "mb-6" }, "Success in both photography and engineering requires a standardized approach. At Northbrand, we follow nine core principles: Prevent Error, Context-Awareness, Limit Obligations, Standardize Systems, System Integrity, Maintain Momentum, Quality over Volume, Force Multipliers, and Externalize Data."),
            React.createElement("blockquote", { className: "border-l-4 border-[#E20613] pl-6 italic text-xl my-10 font-serif" },
                "\"Precision is the foundation of vision.\""
            )
        )
    },
    {
        id: 2,
        title: "Aperture & Integrity",
        date: "April 28, 2025",
        excerpt: "Why the M-series philosophy defines our technical stack.",
        image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1510127034890-ba27508e9f1c?auto=format&fit=crop&q=80&w=1000",
        content: React.createElement(React.Fragment, null,
            React.createElement("p", { className: "mb-6" }, "The Leica M-series camera is the ultimate expression of 'Limit Obligations.' It does one thing perfectly. We apply this to our code: remove the bloat, focus on the core utility, and ensure the UI is as tactile and responsive as a brass dial.")
        )
    }
];

export const BRAND_NAME = 'Northbrand Collective';

entries.description: Removed Community category since Wylie Band Command was removed.
Removed Community from category union type.

payload.text: Review the entire application for accessibility improvements, ensuring proper ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and color contrast, especially in dark mode.

**Initiating Accessibility Review**

I'm beginning a deep dive into application accessibility. My initial focus centers on `index.html`. I'll meticulously examine this file, paying close attention to language attributes and meta tags to ensure a strong foundation for accessibility compliance. This is the starting point for a comprehensive accessibility overhaul.


**Analyzing Component Accessibility**

I've moved beyond the initial index file and I am focusing now on individual components. I am exploring `App.tsx` and verifying each component will manage focus when the view changes, using `useRef` and `useEffect`. I'll explore semantic HTML, ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation in `Navbar.tsx`. I'll also check semantic structure and accessible elements in `Hero.tsx`, `ProductGrid.tsx`, `ProductCard.tsx`, `ProductDetail.tsx`, `Journal.tsx`, `JournalDetail.tsx`, and `Footer.tsx` components. Then I will address issues in About.tsx. Finally, I will check the unused CartDrawer.tsx and Checkout.tsx components for accessibility, even though they appear unused, in case logic changes later.


**Refining Component Focus**

I've examined `App.tsx` further, noticing it *doesn't* actually render unused components. I'll omit unused components from review unless they appear in use. Now, I'm focusing on focus management within `App.tsx`. The goal is to set focus logically when switching views. I'll add a ref to the `main` container, focusing it after each view change to ensure screen readers announce new content. Then I will review `Navbar.tsx`. The `<nav>` element is fine, but the hamburger button needs aria attributes, as do the mobile menu links and theme toggle.


**Assessing Component Accessibility**

I've moved on to examining more components. The `Hero.tsx` and `ProductGrid.tsx` seem sound so far, with a focus on `ProductGrid` filter buttons using `aria-pressed` or `aria-current`. I see some accessibility issues in `ProductCard.tsx` and I'll change the clickable div to a button. I'll also add an `aria-label` to the back button of `ProductDetail.tsx`, and make a similar change to the article cards in `Journal.tsx`, also a button. I will also check the back button of `JournalDetail.tsx` and will ensure semantic headings in `About.tsx` and that the input in `Footer.tsx` has a proper label.


**Evaluating Color Contrast**

I am now assessing color contrast, considering the user's feedback, particularly in dark mode. Initial colors for dark mode are dark grey/brown and off-black. Text is off-white or grey, and links are Leica red. I'll focus on ensuring sufficient contrast, especially for links and interactive elements, possibly using `aria-label` where text might be insufficient. My focus is on the `Join the list` button, and the social media links.


**Addressing Card Navigation**

I'm focused on keyboard navigation. I need to make changes to `ProductCard.tsx` and `Journal.tsx`. My goal is to ensure they are accessible using `button` elements instead of `div` elements, or by adding appropriate ARIA attributes. I'll focus on the interaction for each component. I will update those components with keyboard interaction logic.


**Implementing Interactive Changes**

I've been working on component updates, especially for keyboard navigation. I updated `ProductGrid.tsx` with `aria-pressed` attributes for category buttons. I've also changed the clickable `div` in `ProductCard.tsx` and the article wrapper to an `article` and changed them to `button` elements, and ensured `alt` text for images. In the `Footer.tsx` component, I added an `aria-label` to the email input field. Now, I will update the code for `App.tsx` and ensure its accessibility. I am re-evaluating color contrast ratios, considering those earlier updates.


**Refining Navbar Accessibility**

I'm now focusing on `Navbar.tsx` and incorporating interactive changes based on those previous assessments. I've added an `aria-label` to the theme toggle button for clarity and an `aria-expanded` and `aria-label` attribute to the hamburger menu to reflect its state. I will make sure the changes I've made are keyboard navigable. I am also making sure the code is accessible, especially for keyboard navigation, and focusing on the overall user experience.


**Improving Card Component**

I've been re-examining the `ProductCard.tsx` component. I'm updating it to use an `<article>` with `role="button"` instead of a `div`. I've added keyboard interaction logic with `onKeyDown` and focus management. I removed the redundant `alt` text and only kept it in `aria-label`, now. I'm ensuring this update, with click, keyboard, and screen reader, functions correctly for all users.


**Revising Card Interaction**

I'm now iterating on the card components, specifically `Journal.tsx`, `ProductCard.tsx`, and making some slight changes to `ProductDetail.tsx` and `JournalDetail.tsx`. Added ARIA attributes to buttons and semantic structure for accessibility.

Made cards keyboard interactive with role="button" and tabIndex.
